title: ByteWolf Plugin Capability v0.1
description: One named, versioned unit of function a plugin provides or a runtime may grant. A Capability is a component type, not a standalone document: its own 'version' pins its data contract, so it does not repeat the manifest's contract_version. See docs/workstreams/plugin-sdk.md.

capability_id.description: Namespaced, lower-case identifier, e.g. 'telemetry.read' or 'world_memory.query'. The registry also enforces a namespace allowlist in code; the pattern here keeps ids machine-comparable so a conflict is exact, not a casing accident.

version.description: The capability's own contract version, so a consumer can pin exactly what shape it will receive. Distinct from the plugin's release 'version' and from the manifest 'contract_version'.

access.description: The access class, as a fail-closed positive enum. There is deliberately NO 'actuate' or 'control' value: a flight-control capability cannot even be expressed, so the schema itself rejects it before any registry or allowlist runs. 'read' returns current artifacts, 'query' answers a request over stored data, 'propose' emits a candidate for the admission pipeline, 'communicate' talks to a person or channel. None of these can drive PX4.

data_contract.description: Optional reference to the versioned schema of what this capability returns, e.g. {"name": "observation", "version": "v0.1"}. Ties a capability to an existing data contract rather than restating its shape.
